items.subject: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT
items.body: Workspace agents in ChatGPT are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.

items.subject: Workspace agents
items.body: Learn how to build, use, and scale workspace agents in ChatGPT to automate repeatable workflows, connect tools, and streamline team operations.

items.subject: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
items.body: A deep dive into the Codex agent loop, showing how WebSockets and connection-scoped caching reduced API overhead and improved model latency.

items.subject: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
items.body: OpenAI Privacy Filter is an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information (PII) in text with state-of-the-art accuracy

items.subject: Introducing ChatGPT Images 2.0
items.body: ChatGPT Images 2.0 introduces a state-of-the-art image generation model with improved text rendering, multilingual support, and advanced visual reasoning.

items.subject: Product Hunt Launch: ChatGPT Images 2.0
items.body: First image model with thinking capabilities

items.subject: ChatGPT Images 2.0
items.body: AI image generation with a thinking layer. Create, refine, and validate visuals in one flow. Supports flexible aspect ratios and multiple outputs per prompt, making it easier to go from idea to production-ready assets fast.

items.subject: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
items.body: OpenAI launches Codex Transformation Partners, a program with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle.

items.subject: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
items.body: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

items.subject: Codex for (almost) everything
items.body: The updated Codex app for macOS and Windows adds computer use, in-app browsing, image generation, memory, and plugins to accelerate developer workflows.

items.subject: Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
items.body: OpenAI introduces GPT-Rosalind, a frontier reasoning model built to accelerate drug discovery, genomics analysis, protein reasoning, and scientific research workflows.

summary:
Here are 4-6 bullet points summarizing key developments in the field of AI, along with 1 trend:

**Bullet Points:**

* **OpenAI releases Workspace Agents**: OpenAI has introduced workspace agents in ChatGPT, which are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.
* **ChatGPT Images 2.0**: OpenAI has released ChatGPT Images 2.0, a state-of-the-art image generation model with improved text rendering, multilingual support, and advanced visual reasoning.
* **NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate**: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated to advance agentic and physical AI, with a focus on developing AI-powered applications in areas such as climate, conservation, and disaster monitoring.
* **Hyatt Deploys ChatGPT Enterprise**: Hyatt has deployed ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.
* **Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence**: NVIDIA and Adobe have partnered to develop AI agents that accelerate creative workflows, transforming how work gets done across all industries.

**Trend:** **AI Adoption Accelerates in Industries Beyond Tech**: The recent developments highlight a trend towards AI adoption in industries beyond technology, such as hospitality (Hyatt), manufacturing (NVIDIA and partners), and life sciences (OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind). This trend suggests that AI is becoming increasingly mainstream and is being applied to solve complex problems across various sectors.
